name: Trio
what_it_does: Python async library focused on usability and correctness, built around structured concurrency principles. Trio's nursery concept ensures all child tasks are cleaned up when the parent exits — prevents task leaks common in asyncio. Strict structured concurrency makes concurrent code easier to reason about. Alternative to asyncio for agents needing reliable task lifecycle management. AnyIO provides an abstraction layer over both asyncio and Trio.
use_cases: Build agent systems where reliable task cancellation and cleanup is critical — Trio's nursery guarantees no orphaned tasks
Write concurrent network code in Python where structured concurrency prevents the task leak bugs common in asyncio
Create robust agent pipelines where multiple concurrent operations must all complete or all be cancelled together
Develop Python servers and clients with Trio-native libraries (trio-websocket, httpx with anyio) for clean concurrency
Use AnyIO as an abstraction over asyncio and Trio for libraries that need to support both async backends
not_for: Teams with large asyncio codebases — Trio is not compatible with asyncio libraries without AnyIO bridge
Projects requiring the full asyncio ecosystem (uvloop, aiohttp, asyncpg) — most async Python libraries target asyncio
Simple one-off async scripts — asyncio is sufficient and has broader tooling support
best_when: You're building a new async Python system from scratch and want structured concurrency with reliable task lifecycle management — correctness over ecosystem breadth.
avoid_when: You need asyncio ecosystem compatibility (aiohttp, asyncpg, celery) or are adding async to an existing asyncio codebase.

auth.notes: Python concurrency library. No authentication required.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Trio provides some of the best error messages and debugging support in the Python async ecosystem. Structured concurrency means errors propagate clearly. Trio's exception handling for multi-task errors uses ExceptionGroup (Python 3.11+).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Concurrency library — idempotency is application-level concern. Trio nurseries clean up predictably.
known_agent_gotchas: Trio is not compatible with asyncio — cannot use asyncio libraries (aiohttp, asyncpg) directly; requires AnyIO bridge or trio-native equivalents
trio.run() is the entry point — cannot call Trio functions from synchronous code or asyncio context without explicit adaptation
Nurseries enforce structured concurrency strictly — background tasks must be inside a nursery scope; 'fire and forget' patterns require careful nursery design
Exception handling in nurseries uses ExceptionGroup (Python 3.11+) or trio's BaseExceptionGroup — handling multiple concurrent exceptions requires exceptiongroup library on older Python
Cancellation in Trio uses Cancelled exception and cancel scopes — code must be cancellation-safe; uncancellable operations use trio.CancelScope(shield=True)
Trio's strict structured concurrency prevents some common async patterns — tasks cannot be started outside nurseries which may require architectural changes

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure Python library. No external network calls. Security depends on how it's used, not the library itself.
